name: lcx-doctor
tagline: Diagnoses LazyCodex/Codex CLI install health by syncing the latest official sources and diffing them against the local install.
verdict: Solid diagnostic skill: explicitly forbids mutating the user's install, scopes writes to a temp/source-root cache, quarantines rather than deletes corrupt state, and requires evidence for every verdict. Network calls only pull two named, official upstream repos for comparison; nothing local is sent out. Minor gaps: no explicit 'when not to use' guidance, no

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do NOT use when...' statement in the description; the only negative-trigger logic (don't call `lazycodex doctor` to avoid recursion) is buried in step 4 rather than stated up front.
Script assumes `git`, `gh`, and `codex` CLIs are installed and on PATH but never states this as a prerequisite anywhere in frontmatter or body.
No version field, changelog, or last-updated marker anywhere in the frontmatter or body, despite the skill's entire purpose being to track drift against fast-moving upstream sources.
